message:
feat(fp-io): SqlReadOptions::index_col for unified index_col on read paths (br-c1h9 / fd90.36)

Add index_col: Option<String> to SqlReadOptions matching
pd.read_sql(.., index_col=...) and pd.read_sql_table(table, con,
index_col=...). When Some(name), read_sql_with_options and
read_sql_table_with_options promote that column to the DataFrame
index after row materialization (delegates to existing
promote_column_to_index helper). None preserves the default
RangeIndex. Empty string is rejected with IoError::Sql.

When both options.index_col and the explicit index_col arg of the
*_with_options_and_index_col variants are set, the explicit arg
wins — the function clones options with index_col=None before
delegation to avoid double-promotion. (Documented as: explicit
kwarg always wins, matching pandas semantics where positional /
explicit always overrides defaults.)

Out of scope: list-of-strings for MultiIndex (lands in a separate
slice once row-MultiIndex SQL coverage is in scope). Chunked read
paths still take the explicit index_col arg — options.index_col
is silently ignored on those paths in this slice.

22 existing SqlReadOptions struct literal sites bulk-updated with
index_col: None for backwards compat.

6 new tests cover: index_col=None keeps RangeIndex, Some promotes
named column, missing column errors with 'not present' message,
empty string rejected, columns+index_col combination produces
projected-then-promoted frame, explicit-arg-wins-over-options
verified by setting both and confirming the arg's choice survives.

385 fp-io tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean across fp-io +
umbrella; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
